samples.xml_description:
Implement a 16-bit divider module, the dividend is 16-bit and the divider is 8-bit in combinational logic. Extract the higher bits of the dividend, matching the bit width of the divisor. Compare these bits with the divisor: if the dividend bits are greater, set the quotient to 1, otherwise set it to 0, and use the difference as the remainder. Concatenate the remainder with the highest remaining 1-bit of the dividend, and repeat the process until all dividend bits are processed. 16-bit dividend. 8-bit divisor. 16-bit quotient. 16-bit remainder. The parameter i represents the index of the current bit of dividend being compared with the divisor. The parameter rem represents the remainder obtained by subtracting the divisor from the dividend bits.

Initialize two 16-bit registers, a_reg and b_reg, to store the values of A and B.

Initialize a 16-bit register, result_reg, to store the quotient.

Initialize a 16-bit register, odd_reg, to store the remainder.

Initialize a 16-bit register, temp_reg, to store the difference between a_reg and b_reg.

Initialize a 1-bit register, carry_reg, to store the borrow from the subtraction.

Initialize a 16-bit register, mask_reg, to store the mask for the current bit.

Initialize a 1-bit register, zero_reg, to store the flag for the dividend bits being zero.

Initialize a 1-bit register, done_reg, to store the flag for the division being done.

Initialize a 1-bit register, set_reg, to store the flag for the quotient being set.

Initialize a 1-bit register, rem_reg, to store the flag for the remainder being processed.

Initialize a 1-bit register, temp_reg_2, to store the borrow from the subtraction.

Initialize a 16-bit register, temp_reg_1, to store the shifted dividend bits.

Initialize i_reg to 0.

Initialize carry_reg to 0.

Initialize mask_reg to 1.

Initialize zero_reg to 0.

Initialize done_reg to 0.

Initialize set_reg to 0.

Initialize rem_reg to 0.

Initialize temp_reg_2 to 0.

Initialize temp_reg_1 to 0.

While i_reg is less than 16:

  1. Set temp_reg_1 to the bitwise AND of a_reg and mask_reg.
  2. Set temp_reg_2 to the bitwise AND of b_reg and mask_reg.
  3. Set rem_reg to 1.
  4. Set carry_reg to the subtraction of temp_reg_1 and temp_reg_2.
  5. Set temp_reg to the value of carry_reg.
  6. Set a_reg to the bitwise AND of a_reg and ~mask_reg.
  7. Set a_reg to the bitwise OR of a_reg and (temp_reg << 1).
  8. Set zero_reg to the bitwise OR of zero_reg and (rem_reg & ~(|temp_reg_2)).
  9. Set rem_reg to the bitwise AND of rem_reg and (|temp_reg_2).
  10. Set mask_reg to the left shift of mask_reg.
  11. Set i_reg to the addition of i_reg and 1.

Set done_reg to 1.

Set result_reg to the bitwise AND of result_reg and ~zero_reg.

Set odd_reg to the bitwise AND of a_reg and ~zero_reg.

Set result to the value of result_reg.

Set odd to the value of odd_reg.
